info.name: My_Projects
info.description:
# My_Projects — API Test Collection

This collection covers a **complete, end-to-end API testing workflow** built on the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) REST API. It is structured into two focused folders, each demonstrating a distinct testing scenario — from basic authentication flows to a fully automated user lifecycle.

The collection is designed to be run using the **Postman Collection Runner** with the **Environment Testing 2** environment active.

---

## Collection Structure

```
My_Projects
├── Authorization and Authentication/
│   ├── Login                    (POST  {{baseURL}}/api/login)
│   └── Get User                 (GET   {{baseURL}}/api/users?page=2)
│
└── User Flow Automation/
    ├── Login (extract token)    (POST  {{baseUrl}}/api/login)       — Step 1
    ├── Create User              (POST  {{baseUrl}}/api/users)        — Step 2
    ├── Update User              (PUT   {{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}) — Step 3
    └── Delete User              (DELETE {{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}) — Step 4

 ```

---

## Folders Overview

### 1\. Authorization and Authentication

Demonstrates the core login-and-token-reuse pattern. A `Login` request authenticates the user and stores the returned bearer token in `{{token}}`. The `Get User` request then uses that token to make an authenticated call to retrieve a paginated list of users.

### 2\. User Flow Automation

An end-to-end automated flow covering the full user lifecycle:

- **Login** → extracts and stores `{{token}}`
    
- **Create User** → creates a user and stores `{{userId}}`
    
- **Update User** → updates the user using `{{userId}}`
    
- **Delete User** → deletes the user and cleans up `{{userId}}`
    

All steps are chained via environment variables and designed to run sequentially in the Collection Runner.

---

## Environment Variables

This collection uses the **Environment Testing 2** environment. The following variables are required:

| Variable | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{baseUrl}}` | string | Base URL of the API (e.g., `https://reqres.in`) |
| `{{token}}` | secret | Bearer token extracted from the Login response |
| `{{userId}}` | string | User ID extracted from the Create User response (set/unset dynamically) |
| `{{time now}}` | string | Current timestamp set by the Login pre-request script |

---

## Authentication

All authenticated requests use **Bearer Token** authentication sourced from `{{token}}`. All requests also include an `x-api-key` header required by the reqres.in API.

---

## How to Run

1. Select the **Environment Testing 2** environment
    
2. Ensure `{{baseUrl}}` is set to `https://reqres.in`
    
3. Open the **Collection Runner**
    
4. Select the **My_Projects** collection
    
5. Run all requests in order — the scripts will handle token and userId propagation automatically
    

---

## Test Coverage Summary

| Request | Positive Tests | Negative Tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Login (Auth folder) | 8 | 4 |
| Get User | 15 | 6 |
| Login (extract token) | 7 | 4 |
| Create User | 9 | 4 |
| Update User | 9 | 4 |
| Delete User | 6 | 4 |

---

## API Reference

All requests target the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API — a free, open API designed for testing and prototyping.

request.description:
## Get User

**Method:** `GET`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseURL}}/api/users?page=2`  
**Folder:** Authorization and Authentication — Step 2 of 2

---

### Purpose

Retrieves a paginated list of users from the API using **Bearer Token authentication**. The token is dynamically sourced from the `{{token}}` environment variable, which is automatically populated by the preceding Login request. A pre-request script also records the current Unix timestamp in `{{timestamp}}` before the request fires.

---

### Request Details

**Authentication:** Bearer Token — `{{token}}`

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer {{token}}` | Bearer token injected from `{{token}}` environment variable |

**Query Parameters**

| Parameter | Value | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `page` | `2` | Returns the second page of the users list |

---

### Pre-Request Script

Sets the current Unix timestamp (in seconds) into the `{{timestamp}}` environment variable before the request is sent:

```javascript
pm.environment.set("timestamp", Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000));
console.log("timestamp:", Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000));
```

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
    "page": 2,
    "per_page": 6,
    "total": 12,
    "total_pages": 2,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 7,
            "email": "michael.lawson@reqres.in",
            "first_name": "Michael",
            "last_name": "Lawson",
            "avatar": "https://reqres.in/img/faces/7-image.jpg"
        }
    ],
    "support": {
        "url": "https://contentcaddy.io?utm_source=reqres...",
        "text": "..."
    }
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | Current page number |
| `per_page` | integer | Number of records per page |
| `total` | integer | Total number of users |
| `total_pages` | integer | Total number of pages |
| `data` | array | Array of user objects |
| `data[].id` | integer | User ID |
| `data[].email` | string | User email address |
| `data[].first_name` | string | User's first name |
| `data[].last_name` | string | User's last name |
| `data[].avatar` | string | URL to the user's avatar image |
| `support.url` | string | Support/sponsor URL |
| `support.text` | string | Support/sponsor message |

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (15)**
- `✅ Status code is 200 OK`
- `✅ Response time is under 3000ms`
- `✅ Response Content-Type is application/json`
- `✅ Response body is valid JSON`
- `✅ Response contains 'page' property`
- `✅ Page number equals 2 (matches query param)`
- `✅ Response contains 'per_page' property`
- `✅ Response contains 'total' property as a positive number`
- `✅ Response contains 'total_pages' property`
- `✅ Response contains 'data' array`
- `✅ 'data' array is non-empty`
- `✅ Each user object has required fields: id, email, first_name, last_name, avatar`
- `✅ Each user's email is a valid email format`
- `✅ Each user's avatar is a valid URL`
- `✅ Each user's id is a positive integer`
- `✅ Response contains 'support' object with 'url' and 'text'`
- `✅ Bearer token environment variable is set (token not empty)`

**Negative Tests (6)**
- `❌ Status code is NOT 401 Unauthorized`
- `❌ Status code is NOT 403 Forbidden`
- `❌ Status code is NOT 404 Not Found`
- `❌ Status code is NOT 5xx (no server error)`
- `❌ Response body does NOT contain an 'error' property`
- `❌ 'data' array does NOT contain users from page 1 (id 1–6)`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseURL}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | **Read** | Bearer token set by the Login request |
| `{{timestamp}}` | **Written** | Unix timestamp set by the pre-request script |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseURL}}` to be set in the active environment
- Requires `{{token}}` to be populated — run the **Login** request first

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- Page 2 returns user IDs 7–12; the negative test confirms page 1 IDs (1–6) are not present

request.description:
## Login

**Method:** `POST`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseURL}}/api/login`  
**Folder:** Authorization and Authentication — Step 1 of 2

---

### Purpose

Authenticates a user against the reqres.in API using email and password credentials. On success, the returned bearer `token` is automatically extracted by the post-response test script and stored in the `{{token}}` environment variable for use by the subsequent Get User request.

---

### Request Details

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Request body format |
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |

**Body (JSON)**

```json
{
    "email": "eve.holt@reqres.in",
    "password": "cityslicka"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `email` | string | Registered email address |
| `password` | string | User password |

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
    "token": "QpwL5tpe83ilfN2"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `token` | string | Bearer token for authenticated requests |

---

### Post-Response Script (Tests)

The test script performs the following:
1. Validates the response contains a `token` property
2. Stores the token in `{{token}}` via `pm.environment.set("token", jsonData.token)`
3. Logs the token to the Postman console

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (8)**
- `✅ Status code is 200 OK`
- `✅ Response time is under 3000ms`
- `✅ Response Content-Type is application/json`
- `✅ Response body is valid JSON`
- `✅ Response body contains 'token' property`
- `✅ Token is a non-empty string`
- `✅ Token is stored in environment variable`
- `✅ Response body does NOT expose password`

**Negative Tests (4)**
- `❌ Status code is NOT 4xx (no client error on valid credentials)`
- `❌ Status code is NOT 5xx (no server error)`
- `❌ Response body does NOT contain an 'error' property`
- `❌ Token value is NOT null or undefined`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseURL}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | **Written** | Token extracted from response and stored for downstream use |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseURL}}` to be set in the active environment (e.g., `https://reqres.in`)
- Must be run **before** Get User so that `{{token}}` is populated

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- The credentials used (`eve.holt@reqres.in` / `cityslicka`) are test credentials provided by reqres.in

item.description:
## Authorization and Authentication

This folder covers the **core authentication and authorized data-access flow** for the project. It contains two requests that work together in sequence to demonstrate a complete login-to-data-retrieval workflow.

---

### Folder Overview

| Step | Request | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|------|---------|--------|----------|---------| 
| 1 | Login | `POST` | `{{baseURL}}/api/login` | Authenticate and extract bearer token |
| 2 | Get User | `GET` | `{{baseURL}}/api/users?page=2` | Retrieve paginated user list using the token |

---

### Requests in this Folder

#### 1. Login — `POST {{baseURL}}/api/login`

Authenticates a user using email and password credentials. On success, the returned `token` is automatically extracted by the post-response test script and stored in the `{{token}}` environment variable for use in subsequent requests.

**Test Coverage:** 8 positive tests, 4 negative tests

#### 2. Get User — `GET {{baseURL}}/api/users?page=2`

Retrieves a paginated list of users from the API using Bearer Token authentication. The token is sourced dynamically from `{{token}}`. A pre-request script also sets a `{{timestamp}}` environment variable before the request fires.

**Test Coverage:** 15 positive tests, 6 negative tests

---

### Environment Variables Used

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseURL}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | Written (Login) / Read (Get User) | Bearer token extracted from Login response |
| `{{timestamp}}` | Written (Get User pre-request) | Unix timestamp set before the Get User request fires |

---

### How to Run

Run the requests **in order** (Login first, then Get User). The Login request's test script automatically populates `{{token}}`, which Get User then uses for authentication. No manual variable setup is needed beyond setting `{{baseURL}}` in the active environment.

---

### Notes

- Both requests target the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- All requests include an `x-api-key` header required by reqres.in
- This folder is self-contained and can be run independently of the User Flow Automation folder

request.description:
## Login (extract token)

**Method:** `POST`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/api/login`  
**Folder:** User Flow Automation — Step 1 of 4

---

### Purpose

Authenticates the user against the reqres.in API using email and password credentials. On success, extracts the returned bearer `token` and stores it in the environment variable `{{token}}` for use by subsequent requests (Create User, Update User, Delete User). A pre-request script also records the current local timestamp in `{{time now}}`.

---

### Request Details

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Request body format |
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |

**Body (JSON)**

```json
{
    "email": "eve.holt@reqres.in",
    "password": "cityslicka"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `email` | string | Registered email address |
| `password` | string | User password |

---

### Pre-Request Script

Computes the current local timestamp and stores it in `{{time now}}` (format: `MM-DD-YYYY HH:MM:SS`):

```javascript
(function(){
    const pad = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, '0');
    const d = new Date();
    const formatted = `${pad(d.getMonth()+1)}-${pad(d.getDate())}-${d.getFullYear()} ${pad(d.getHours())}:${pad(d.getMinutes())}:${pad(d.getSeconds())}`;
    pm.environment.set("time now", formatted);
    console.log("pre-req : time now =", pm.environment.get("time now"));
})();
```

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
    "token": "QpwL5tpe83ilfN2"
}
```

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (7)**
- `Response has 'token' property` — Validates token exists and stores it in `{{token}}`
- `Status code is 200`
- `Response time is under 3000ms`
- `Content-Type is application/json`
- `Token is a non-empty string`
- `Token is stored in environment variable`
- `Response body is a valid JSON object`

**Negative / Edge-Case Tests (4)**
- `Response does NOT contain 'error' field on success`
- `Token value is not 'null' or 'undefined' string`
- `Response does not expose sensitive password field`
- `Status code is not 4xx or 5xx`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseUrl}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | **Written** | Bearer token extracted from response |
| `{{time now}}` | **Written** | Current timestamp set by pre-request script |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseUrl}}` to be set in the active environment (e.g., `https://reqres.in`)
- Must be run **first** in the User Flow Automation sequence

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- The credentials used (`eve.holt@reqres.in` / `cityslicka`) are test credentials provided by reqres.in

pm.test("Status code is not 4xx or 5xx", function () {
pm.test("Response body is not an empty object", function () {
pm.test("'id' field is not null or undefined", function () {

request.description:
## Create User

**Method:** `POST`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/api/users`  
**Folder:** User Flow Automation — Step 2 of 4

---

### Purpose

Creates a new user on the reqres.in API using the bearer token obtained from the Login step. On success, extracts the returned `id` and stores it in the environment variable `{{userId}}` for use by the Update User and Delete User requests.

---

### Request Details

**Authentication:** Bearer Token — `{{token}}` (set by Login step)

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Request body format |
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |

**Body (JSON)**

```json
{
    "name": "Suraj",
    "job": "Automation Engineer"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Name of the user to create |
| `job` | string | Job title of the user |

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `201 Created`

```json
{
    "name": "Suraj",
    "job": "Automation Engineer",
    "id": "123",
    "createdAt": "2026-05-13T11:00:00.000Z"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Name of the created user |
| `job` | string | Job title of the created user |
| `id` | string | Unique ID assigned to the new user |
| `createdAt` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of creation |

---

### Post-Response Script

The test script extracts the `id` from the response and stores it in `{{userId}}`:

```javascript
const idVal = body.id;
pm.environment.set("userId", String(idVal));
console.log("userId set to:", pm.environment.get("userId"));
```

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (9)**
- `Response is valid JSON and contains 'id'` — Validates JSON structure, confirms `id` exists, stores it in `{{userId}}`
- `Status code is 201 Created`
- `Response time is under 3000ms`
- `Content-Type is application/json`
- `Response contains 'name' field matching request` — Confirms `name` equals `"Suraj"`
- `Response contains 'job' field matching request` — Confirms `job` equals `"Automation Engineer"`
- `Response contains 'createdAt' timestamp`
- `userId environment variable is set and non-empty`
- `User ID is a non-empty string`

**Negative / Edge-Case Tests (4)**
- `Response does NOT contain 'error' field on success`
- `Status code is not 4xx or 5xx`
- `Response body is not an empty object`
- `'id' field is not null or undefined`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseUrl}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | Read | Bearer token set by Login step |
| `{{userId}}` | **Written** | User ID extracted from response, used by Update and Delete steps |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseUrl}}` to be set in the active environment
- Requires `{{token}}` to be set — run **Login (extract token)** first

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- reqres.in simulates user creation but does not persist data between requests

exec: pm.test("Response body is a valid JSON object", function () {
pm.test("'updatedAt' is a valid ISO 8601 date string", function () {
pm.test("userId environment variable is still set", function () {
pm.test("Response does NOT contain 'error' field on success", function () {
pm.test("Status code is not 4xx or 5xx", function () {
pm.test("Response body is not empty", function () {
pm.test("Response does not return stale/old name", function () {

request.description:
## Update User

**Method:** `PUT`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}`  
**Folder:** User Flow Automation — Step 3 of 4

---

### Purpose

Updates the details of the user created in the previous step. Uses the `{{userId}}` environment variable (set by Create User) to target the correct user, and the `{{token}}` bearer token for authentication. Validates that the API correctly reflects the updated `name` and `Job` fields, and returns an `updatedAt` timestamp.

---

### Request Details

**Authentication:** Bearer Token — `{{token}}` (set by Login step)

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Request body format |
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |

**Body (JSON)**

```json
{
    "name": "Suraj DP",
    "Job": "QA Automation Engineer"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Updated name for the user |
| `Job` | string | Updated job title (note: capital J as expected by the API) |

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
    "name": "Suraj DP",
    "Job": "QA Automation Engineer",
    "updatedAt": "2026-05-13T11:00:00.000Z"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Updated name |
| `Job` | string | Updated job title |
| `updatedAt` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of the update |

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (9)**
- `Status code is 200`
- `Response time is under 3000ms`
- `Content-Type is application/json`
- `Response body is a valid JSON object`
- `Response contains updated 'name' field` — Confirms `name` equals `"Suraj DP"`
- `Response contains updated 'Job' field` — Confirms `Job` equals `"QA Automation Engineer"`
- `Response contains 'updatedAt' timestamp`
- `'updatedAt' is a valid ISO 8601 date string`
- `userId environment variable is still set` — Confirms `{{userId}}` persists for the Delete step

**Negative / Edge-Case Tests (4)**
- `Response does NOT contain 'error' field on success`
- `Status code is not 4xx or 5xx`
- `Response body is not empty`
- `Response does not return stale/old name` — Confirms `name` is not the old value `"Suraj"`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseUrl}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | Read | Bearer token set by Login step |
| `{{userId}}` | Read | User ID set by Create User step, used in URL path |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseUrl}}` to be set in the active environment
- Requires `{{token}}` to be set — run **Login (extract token)** first
- Requires `{{userId}}` to be set — run **Create User** first

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- Note the capital `J` in `Job` — this matches the field name expected and returned by the reqres.in API
- reqres.in simulates the update but does not persist changes

pm.test("Status code confirms successful deletion (204 No Content)", function () {

pm.test("Status code is not 403 (not forbidden)", function () {
pm.test("Status code is not 500 (no server error)", function () {
// Clean up: clear userId from environment after deletion
console.log('userId cleared from environment after deletion.');

request.description:
## Delete User

**Method:** `DELETE`  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}`  
**Folder:** User Flow Automation — Step 4 of 4

---

### Purpose

Deletes the user created in Step 2 using the `{{userId}}` environment variable. Validates that the API returns a `204 No Content` response with an empty body, confirming successful deletion. After all tests run, the `{{userId}}` environment variable is cleaned up (unset) to leave the environment in a clean state for the next run.

---

### Request Details

**Authentication:** Bearer Token — `{{token}}` (set by Login step)

**Headers**

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `x-api-key` | `pub_cefb...` | API key required by reqres.in |

**Body:** None — DELETE requests do not require a body

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `204 No Content`  
**Body:** *(empty)*

---

### Post-Test Cleanup

After all tests run, the test script unsets `{{userId}}` from the environment:

```javascript
pm.environment.unset('userId');
console.log('userId cleared from environment after deletion.');
```

This ensures the environment is clean for the next Collection Runner execution.

---

### Test Coverage

**Positive Tests (6)**
- `Status code is 204` — Confirms successful deletion (No Content)
- `Response time is under 3000ms`
- `Response body is empty (no content on delete)`
- `Status code confirms successful deletion (204 No Content)`
- `userId environment variable was set before deletion` — Confirms `{{userId}}` was available
- `Response does not return a body with error` — If any body exists, it must not contain `error`

**Negative / Edge-Case Tests (4)**
- `Status code is not 404 (user existed before delete)`
- `Status code is not 401 (request was authorized)`
- `Status code is not 403 (not forbidden)`
- `Status code is not 500 (no server error)`

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Direction | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `{{baseUrl}}` | Read | Base URL of the API |
| `{{token}}` | Read | Bearer token set by Login step |
| `{{userId}}` | Read then **Unset** | User ID used in URL path; cleared after deletion |

---

### Dependencies

- Requires `{{baseUrl}}` to be set in the active environment
- Requires `{{token}}` to be set — run **Login (extract token)** first
- Requires `{{userId}}` to be set — run **Create User** first

---

### Notes

- This request targets the [reqres.in](https://reqres.in) hosted REST API
- This is the final step in the User Flow Automation sequence
- After this request, `{{userId}}` is automatically removed from the environment, making the flow safe to re-run immediately

item.description:
## User Flow Automation

This folder contains a **complete end-to-end automated test flow** for a User Management API powered by [reqres.in](https://reqres.in). It covers the full lifecycle of a user — authentication, creation, update, and deletion — all chained together using Postman environment variables and the Collection Runner.

---

### Test Flow

The requests must be executed **in sequence**:

| Step | Request | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|------|---------|--------|----------|---------| 
| 1 | Login (extract token) | `POST` | `{{baseUrl}}/api/login` | Authenticate and store `{{token}}` |
| 2 | Create User | `POST` | `{{baseUrl}}/api/users` | Create a user and store `{{userId}}` |
| 3 | Update User | `PUT` | `{{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}` | Update the created user's details |
| 4 | Delete User | `DELETE` | `{{baseUrl}}/api/users/{{userId}}` | Delete the user and clean up `{{userId}}` |

---

### Environment Variable Flow

```
Login (extract token)
  └─ sets {{token}}
       └─ used by: Create User, Update User, Delete User

Create User
  └─ sets {{userId}}
       └─ used by: Update User, Delete User

Delete User
  └─ unsets {{userId}}  ← environment cleanup

Login (extract token) pre-request
  └─ sets {{time now}}  ← current timestamp (MM-DD-YYYY HH:MM:SS)
```

---

### Test Coverage Summary

| Request | Positive Tests | Negative Tests | Total |
|---------|---------------|----------------|-------|
| Login (extract token) | 7 | 4 | 11 |
| Create User | 9 | 4 | 13 |
| Update User | 9 | 4 | 13 |
| Delete User | 6 | 4 | 10 |
| **Total** | **31** | **16** | **47** |

---

### How to Run

1. Ensure the **Environment Testing 2** environment is active
2. Set `{{baseUrl}}` to `https://reqres.in`
3. Open the **Collection Runner**, select this folder (or the full collection)
4. Run all 4 requests in order — scripts handle all variable propagation automatically
5. After the run, `{{userId}}` will be automatically cleared from the environment

---

### Notes

- All requests use **Bearer Token** authentication via `{{token}}`
- All requests include the `x-api-key` header required by reqres.in
- The flow is idempotent: each run creates a fresh user, updates it, then deletes it
- The Login pre-request script records the run start time in `{{time now}}`
